name: BSCE-GRA: Brier Score Calibration via Gradient Rescaling for XGBoost/LightGBM/CatBoost

source_paper.title: Uncertainty Weighted Gradients for Model Calibration

technique: Apply BSCE-GRA (Brier Score Calibration via Gradient Rescaling Adjustments) as a custom objective function in XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost on all 6 HF evolution islands. BSCE-GRA re-weights each sample's gradient update proportional to its per-sample Brier uncertainty, so that samples where the model is most miscalibrated receive amplified gradient corrections. Published CVPR 2025. Code available. Key distinction from focal loss (not in our pipeline): BSCE-GRA uses the full probability vector (all logits) to compute Brier uncertainty, not a single logit. This is more accurate for binary classification where p and (1-p) both carry information.
novelty_vs_pipeline: None of P001-P008 modify the training objective of the tree models directly. P001 changes TabICL's native loss (different model). P006 is post-hoc calibration (MCBoost). P007 applies monotone constraints (not loss modification). BSCE-GRA targets the gradient computation inside XGBoost/LightGBM/CatBoost — a fundamentally different intervention point. This is the first proposal to modify the gradient boosting objective itself for calibration.

mechanism.bsce_gra_grad: brier_weight_i = (p_i - y_i)^2  |  grad_i = (p_i - y_i) * (1 + lambda * brier_weight_i)
mechanism.lambda: Hyperparameter 0.5-2.0 (start at 1.0). Controls strength of Brier-weighted correction.
mechanism.intuition: A sample with p=0.6, y=1 has brier_weight=0.16. Its gradient is amplified by 1.16x. A well-calibrated sample (p=0.9, y=1) has brier_weight=0.01, barely amplified. Miscalibrated samples get stronger pull toward truth.

improvement_rationale: CVPR 2025 experiments show 2-5% ECE reduction across classification benchmarks. Tree models have less natural miscalibration than neural nets (gradient boosting already implicitly regularizes), so we apply a conservative 15% scaling from published neural net results. The 6-island fleet amplifies this: one island canary, measure, then roll out. NBA prediction is a calibration-hard problem (probabilities cluster near 0.5-0.7), which is exactly the regime BSCE-GRA improves most.
files_to_modify: hf-space/app.py (add bsce_gra_objective function, pass to xgb.train as obj=)
hf-space/app.py (add lgbm fobj parameter for LightGBM islands)
hf-space/app.py (CatBoost uses custom_loss parameter with Brier weighting)
implementation_steps: Step 1 (30 min): Add BSCE-GRA custom objective to hf-space/app.py
Step 2 (15 min): In XGBoost training call, replace 'objective': 'binary:logistic' with obj=bsce_gra_objective. Remove objective from params dict.
Step 3 (15 min): For LightGBM, implement as fobj= parameter. For CatBoost, wrap as custom loss object.
Step 4 (30 min): Deploy to S13 (CatBoost specialist) first as canary — measure generation Brier vs baseline over 3 generations.
Step 5 (15 min): If S13 shows improvement (delta < 0), update deploy_island.py default and redeploy all 6 islands.
Step 6: Add lambda=1.0 as a GA-evolvable hyperparameter in the search space (range 0.1-3.0, step 0.1).

canary_deployment.rollout_trigger: 3 generations showing Brier delta < 0 vs S13 baseline

dependencies: None — pure objective function change, no new data required. Works with current 6253 features and existing GA selection.
risk_analysis: Custom objectives in XGBoost/LightGBM are well-supported and reversible. The hessian modification is mild (same order of magnitude as log-loss hessian). Risk: if lambda too high (>3), gradients may destabilize. Mitigate by capping lambda at 2.0 in initial deployment.
complementarity: Additive with P001 (TabICLv2), P002 (Venn-Abers), P006 (MCBoost). These operate on different interventions: P009 changes training objective of tree models; P002 applies post-hoc calibration; P006 applies subgroup correction. All 3 can stack. P009 + P002 is likely the strongest combination (train better, then calibrate post-hoc).

fleet_applicability.S10_xgboost: Direct: replace binary:logistic objective with bsce_gra_objective
fleet_applicability.S12_extratrees: N/A — ExtraTrees does not have a custom gradient objective. Skip for this island.
fleet_applicability.S13_catboost: Canary deployment. CatBoost custom loss via class-based interface.

expected_portfolio_brier.note: P009 is additive with P001 (TabICL) and P002 (Venn-Abers) — they operate on different models/stages
